content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Give an example of each of the following, with some explanation.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: A solvable group of order greater than 10.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: A finite group having a non-cyclic abelian proper subgroup.

body.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group. Suppose that \(N\) is a normal subgroup of even order of \(G\) such that the non-trivial elements of \(N\) form a single conjugacy class of \(G\). Prove that \(N\) is abelian.
Let \(G=A_5\) be the alternating group on 5 letters. Prove that \(G\) is a simple group.
Let \(G=D_{2n}\) be the dihedral group of order \(2n\). Suppose that \(n\geq 4\) is a power of 2. Prove that \(G\) is nilpotent and describe the nilpotency class of \(G\).
Let \(G\) be a group of order 30. Prove that \(G\) has a normal subgroup of order 15.
State and prove Sylow’s First Theorem.
